preamble: The shift from the voiced uvular fricative [ʁ] to the palatal glide [j] (represented here as 'y') is a form of palatalization and fronting, often documented in historical chain shifts or dialectal transitions between back and front dorsal sounds.

examples.note: Proposed correspondences in historical reconstructions where uvular sounds fronted toward palatal or velar glides.
Modeled phonetic shift in constructed language frameworks where dorsal sounds move forward in the vocal tract.
